claim: Interpretability is fundamentally a 'science of deep learning' approach, not just post-hoc poking—it should inform training, data curation, and intentional model design
actionable: Apply interpretability techniques throughout the ML lifecycle (training, post-training, evaluation) rather than only using it for post-hoc analysis
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: SAEs (Sparse Autoencoders) are useful but have limitations—raw activation probes sometimes outperform SAE-based probes in real-world tasks like hallucination detection
actionable: Validate that SAE-based interpretability techniques actually improve downstream task performance; don't assume unsupervised feature discovery is optimal for supervised goals
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Post-training is where unintended side effects emerge (overly sycophantic models, reward hacking), and interpretability can surgically edit behaviors without full retraining
actionable: Use interpretability (steering, activation manipulation) to remove specific undesired behaviors learned during RLHF without expensive retraining
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Steering can remove specific model behaviors (political bias, hallucinations, undesired styles) by editing internal activation vectors in real-time
actionable: Identify problematic features in model activations and apply steering vectors to suppress them while preserving other capabilities
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: In production deployments, synthetic-to-real transfer of interpretability models is hard—you can't train on real PII but must hope synthetic data probes generalize
actionable: When building interpretability-based systems in production, test synthetic methods thoroughly on real data; validate transfer before deploying
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Multilingual and token-level classification requirements break idealized interpretability approaches—Japanese tokenization quirks cause unexpected bugs
actionable: When applying interpretability techniques across languages, test token-level behavior and account for language-specific quirks (tokenization, encoding)
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Real-time steering of trillion-parameter models is now practical with custom-hosted infrastructure (H100 nodes), enabling live behavior editing
actionable: If you need real-time interpretability-driven steering, budget for inference infrastructure (H100 GPUs) to handle latency requirements
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Interpretability methods are computationally inexpensive compared to pre-training—training SAEs, probes, and steering experiments cost thousands of dollars, not millions
actionable: If you want to experiment with interpretability research, do so—the barrier to entry is low (SAE training, probing, steering are all affordable)
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Activation steering is formally equivalent to in-context learning in the limit—you can convert between prompt-based jailbreaks and steering vectors mathematically
actionable: Understand that steering vectors and prompts achieve similar effects; steering is like applying permanent context at the activation level
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Hallucination detection via interpretability requires supervised learning on hallucinations (probes) rather than hoping unsupervised SAEs discover hallucination features
actionable: To detect hallucinations interpretably, train supervised probes on annotated examples rather than relying on unsupervised feature discovery
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Temperature=0 does not solve hallucination—models have internal uncertainty/pleasing-behavior features that drive hallucinations regardless of decoding temperature
actionable: Don't rely on temperature tuning to eliminate hallucinations; instead, use interpretability to understand and suppress the internal representations driving hallucinations
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Feature attribution via SAEs can suffer from feature splitting (one concept split across features) and feature absorption (multiple concepts in one feature)
actionable: When using SAE-discovered features, validate that they represent clean, single concepts; don't assume one SAE feature = one behavioral concept
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: The current AI development lifecycle is 'deeply broken' because we only control data and use RLHF/fine-tuning as a 'straw'—interpretability enables surgical, intentional design
actionable: Move beyond brute-force data and RLHF; adopt interpretability-guided approaches to intentionally design model behaviors from understanding their internals
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Interpretability can extract novel scientific discoveries—models trained on genomics/medical imaging data have discovered biomarkers humans didn't know about
actionable: Use interpretability on domain-specific models (genomics, medical imaging, materials science) to extract novel scientific insights and biomarkers
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: Models trained on physics can learn correct laws or learn 'alien' heuristics that are functionally equivalent but conceptually wrong—interpretability reveals which
actionable: Verify that models don't just achieve good loss curves; use interpretability to confirm they've learned the right underlying concepts, not spurious correlations
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: World models (visual/video models) are easier to interpret than language models because you can directly visualize learned concepts
actionable: If building interpretability tools, prioritize vision/world models first—their interpretability is more tractable and immediately verifiable
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design

claim: SAE-based approaches work better with noisy data than clean data—unsupervised methods get more signal from noisy inputs
actionable: If you have noisy training data for interpretability, SAE-based methods may outperform supervised probes; if data is clean, supervised methods often win
source_title: Goodfire AI's Bet: Interpretability as the Next Frontier of Model Design
